name: Castillo de Valdecorneja, puente del Tormes y Puerto de Umbrías — circular desde El Barco de Ávila
region_intro: Touring cycling around El Barco de Ávila offers routes through the Tormes Valley and towards the Sierra de Gredos mountain range. The region features a mix of terrains, from the gentler landscapes of the valley to the more challenging ascents in the foothills of the Sierra de Gredos. Cyclists can encounter quiet country roads, chestnut and oak forests, and views of the Tormes River. The historic town of El Barco de Ávila, with its 12th-century walls and Valdecorneja Castle, serves as a starting point for many routes. Best touring cycling routes around El Barco de Ávila The most popular touring cycling route is Castillo de Valdecorneja, puente del Tormes y Puerto de Umbrías — circular desde El Barco de Ávila, a 17.6 miles (28.3 km) trail that takes 1 hour 52 minutes to complete. This moderate route passes by Valdecorneja Castle and crosses the Tormes bridge. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Valdecorneja Castle – El Barco de Ávila loop from Bohoyo, a moderate 16.5 miles (26.6 km) path. This route offers views of the Valdecorneja Castle area and the surrounding countryside. Local touring cyclists also love the El Barco de Ávila – Valdecorneja Castle loop from El Barco de Ávila, a 48.2 miles (77.6 km) trail leading through the Valdecorneja Castle area, often completed in about 5 hours 19 minutes. Touring cycling around El Barco de Ávila is defined by the Tormes Valley, the foothills of the Sierra de Gredos, and quiet country roads. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate rides to more difficult routes with significant elevation gains. The routes in El Barco de Ávila are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from more than 20 reviews. More than 100 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore El Barco de Ávila's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available in El Barco de Ávila?
A: There are over 20 touring cycling routes around El Barco de Ávila, offering a variety of experiences for different skill levels. You'll find options ranging from moderate rides to more challenging routes with significant elevation gains.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect when touring cycling around El Barco de Ávila?
A: The region offers a diverse landscape for touring cyclists. You can expect routes through the gentle &lt;p&gt;Tormes Valley&lt;/p&gt; with quiet country roads, as well as more challenging ascents into the foothills of the majestic &lt;p&gt;Sierra de Gredos&lt;/p&gt;. The terrain includes chestnut and oak forests, and you'll often encounter views of the Tormes River.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes in the area?
A: Yes, there are several circular routes available. For example, you can explore the area on the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1105007284/castillo-de-valdecorneja-puente-del-tormes-y-puerto-de-umbrias-circular-desde-el-barco-de-avila" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de Valdecorneja, puente del Tormes y Puerto de Umbrías — circular desde El Barco de Ávila&lt;/a&gt;, which is a moderate 28.3 km ride. Another option is the longer &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/6910766" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Barco de Ávila – Valdecorneja Castle loop from El Barco de Ávila&lt;/a&gt;, a challenging 77.6 km route.

Q: What historical landmarks can I see while cycling in El Barco de Ávila?
A: Many routes pass by significant historical sites. You can cycle past the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3287205" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Valdecorneja Castle&lt;/a&gt;, a 15th-century fortress, and cross the ancient &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5437032" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tormes Bridge&lt;/a&gt;, which has Roman origins. The historic town of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4516184" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Barco de Ávila&lt;/a&gt; itself, with its 12th-century walls, is a point of interest.

Q: Are there routes suitable for beginners or those seeking an easier ride?
A: While there are no routes specifically graded as 'easy' in the immediate selection, there are 10 moderate routes that offer a less strenuous experience compared to the more difficult options. These moderate routes provide a good balance for those looking for an enjoyable ride without extreme challenges.

Q: Are there challenging routes for experienced touring cyclists?
A: Absolutely. For experienced touring cyclists, there are 13 difficult routes available. These routes often feature significant elevation gains, especially as they venture towards the Sierra de Gredos foothills. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/6910766" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Barco de Ávila – Valdecorneja Castle loop from El Barco de Ávila&lt;/a&gt;, which covers 77.6 km with over 1300 meters of elevation gain.

Q: What do other touring cyclists say about the routes in El Barco de Ávila?
A: The touring cycling routes around El Barco de Ávila are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 20 reviews. More than 100 touring cyclists have explored the varied terrain, often praising the scenic views and the mix of valley and mountain landscapes.

Q: Are there any natural viewpoints or scenic spots along the routes?
A: Yes, the region is rich in natural beauty. Many routes offer breathtaking views, particularly as you gain elevation towards the Sierra de Gredos. You can find scenic spots like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6138174" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Views of the Tormes River Valley&lt;/a&gt; and enjoy the landscapes featuring chestnut and oak forests.

Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling in El Barco de Ávila?
A: The region is generally pleasant for cycling. Spring and autumn offer mild temperatures, making them ideal for exploring. Summer can be warm, especially in the Tormes Valley, but higher elevations in the Sierra de Gredos provide cooler conditions. Winter can bring colder weather, particularly in the mountains.

Q: Can I find parking for my touring cycling trip in El Barco de Ávila?
A: El Barco de Ávila, being a town that serves as a starting point for many routes, generally offers parking options within or near the town center. It's advisable to check local parking regulations upon arrival, especially during peak seasons.

Q: Are there family-friendly touring cycling routes in El Barco de Ávila?
A: While specific 'family-friendly' routes are not explicitly listed, the moderate routes in the Tormes Valley, with their gentler landscapes and quiet country roads, could be suitable for families looking for a less challenging ride. Always check the route details and elevation profiles to ensure it matches your family's ability.

Q: Can I reach the cycling routes using public transport?
A: El Barco de Ávila is connected by public transport, which can serve as a starting point for your cycling adventures. For specific routes, you would need to check the local bus or train schedules and their policies regarding transporting bicycles. For more general information about the region, you can visit &lt;a href="https://www.spain.info/en/destination/el-barco-avila/" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;spain.info&lt;/a&gt;.
tips: Valdecorneja Castle, located in El Barco de Ávila, is a medieval gem that dominates the Tormes River valley from a strategic, elevated position. It is one of the most emblematic monuments in the province of Ávila and a must-see for history and architecture lovers.
Riding a bike through the port of Umbrías, it is worthwhile observing the views offered by this wonderful road located in the Sierra de Gredos Regional Park.

Also known as Puente Viejo, the picturesque Tormes bridge allows us to cross the river of the same name when we ride through the town of El Barco de Ávila. The bridge is of Roman origin and reformed in medieval times.

waypoints.name: Puente Viejo of El Barco de Ávila
